entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 6: SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
This sixth post in my series gives an overview of latest UX innovations in SAP Fieldglass, for managing your external workforce. The design of the supplier home page has been updated, and a number of further design updates have been made for a consistent SAP Business Suite experience. On-boarding is now made easy with WalkMe. Joule supports further use cases now, and also supports multiple languages, not just English. We have some nice embedded AI use cases, creating a better user experience, as well as many improvements in details that make a difference in day-to-day work.

In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

I’ve grouped the UX innovations into these main sections:

  • Updated Supplier Home Page.
  • Design Updates for Suite Consistency.
  • Joule Innovotions.
  • Embedded AI for improved UX.
  • WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding.
  • Selected Usability Improvements in Applications
  • Further Information

 

Redesigned Supplier Home Page

The supplier home page is the first place that suppliers go to when using SAP Fieldglass, and hence it is crucial that it provides the most important information, actions and navigation options. Based on thorough user research, we have now redesigned the page to deliver a more streamlined and user-centered experience. At the same time, the new design also supports our focus on providing a consistent user experience across all products within the SAP Business Suite, as described in the next section.

Figure 1 shows an example. As you can see, the Announcement card is prominently visible. Users can choose to close it, in which case a button appears allowing them to re-open. The short video in Figure 2 shows what this looks like on a different example page.

You can configure the layout of the redesigned home page for each role with the Layout Manager, which was introduced in the May 2025 release. This learning journey tells you how to do it.

Figure 1: The redesigned supplier home page in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screen image showing a welcome banner at the top, and a collapsed vertical navigation bar running down the left side. The main screen has a section with pinned links, below which a selection of various cards are shown such as a list of My Work Items, Active Job Postings, Announcements. Two cards have an illustrated message that they have no data to display.

Figure 2: Hiding and re-opening the Announcement card. Alt Text: a short video showing the top half of the home page and the user clicking on “Close” and then “View Announcement”.

Find out more:

  • Smooth and Superior: The New, User-Centered Supplier Home Page.
  • Documentation: Redesigned Supplier Home Page.

 

Design Updates for Suite Consistency

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite, and which are now also available with SAP Fieldglass. As I mentioned in my April blog post Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web), consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises.

In this section, we’ll look at these updates (the first two cover the design updates for consistency):

  • Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu.
  • Redesigned user settings.
  • Design updates for more consistent applications.

Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu

Figure 1 above already features the new shell bar, with the collapsed side navigation bar on the left, showing a vertical list of icons representing menu entries. Users can expand the menu to better understand what each menu icon represents, as shown in Figure 3. This is done by clicking on the icon in the top left corner to the left of the SAP logo. It stays expanded until the user closes it, by clicking on the same icon again.

Figure 3 also shows the redesigned user menu, which follows the design for all SAP Business Suite products.

Figure 3: Navigation menu expanded, new user menu. Alt Text: screenshot of the home pages with the navigation menu on the left showing titles next to the icons, the user menu showing two entries “Settings” and “Legal”, and a separate “Sign Out” option at the bottom.

Redesigned User Settings

Selecting Settings in the user menu shown in Figure 3 above brings you to the redesigned settings page, shown in Figure 4 below. This design follows the SAP Fiori design guidelines, thus providing additional UX consistency across the suite. Beyond these visual aspects, it consolidates fields from the My Profile page and the My Preferences page into a single unified Settings page, which is easily accessed via the user menu. Some of the categories have been renamed too. Find out more.

  • Documentation: Updates to Settings Page.

Figure 4: Redesigned and consolidated user settings. Alt Text: Screenshot showing setttings, with full width cards “User Information” and “Appearance”, each with an “Edit” button top right.

Design Updates for More Consistent Applications.

SAP Fieldglass has also introduced the new illustrations from the SAP Design System (SAP Fiori). You can see some examples in Figures 1 and 2 above.

In addition to that, we have also aligned the design of individual applications with the design system. For example, the decision form list view now has the filters at the top of the page, and the Apply Filter button has been renamed to Go, thus aligning terminology – see Figure 5.

Figure 5: Decision form list view filters and Go button. Alt Text: Screen snippet showing “Decision Forms” above two rows of six and three filters. On the far right a highlighted “Go” button.

We have also moved the icons for table actions to the top of the decision form table, again for more consistency. Find out more in the documentation:

  • Decision Form List View UI Updates.

We have made similar updates in other applications, such as the project list view. You can find the full list in the What’s New documentaiton:

  • Project List View User Interface Updates
  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.

 

Joule innovotions

Now, you can use Joule:

  • in many languages, not just English;
  • to recommend job posting templates when create job postings;
  • to approve decision forms, including legal entity recognition.

Joule: Multi-Language Support

You can use Joule in all the languages available in SAP Fieldglass execept for Mongolian, which is currently not available in Joule. Figure 6 shows an example using French, which is used in many countries, not just France.

Figure 6: Joule multi-language support, showing an example in French. Alt Text: Three Joule images starting with a list of suggested prompts, the user chose “Créer une offre d’emploi”, then showing how Joule asks for details, the user entered “chef de projet”, Joule offered three templates, the user chose one and Joule shows the details. In the background a screenshot showing user preferences with the language French selected.

Not only can you interact with Joule in your native tongue, you can also use Joule to find job posting templates and statement of work templates that your company has translated into your language.

For companies that translate job postings and statement of work templates into multiple languages, Joule can automatically detect the user's preferred language and retrieve the corresponding localized template, if available. If not available in the user's preferred language, templates in the company-defined language will be returned. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Translated Template Retrieval in Joule.

Joule: Job Posting Template Recommendations

If you need to create a job posting, Joule can make this easier for you: after stating your requirements for the job, Joule will recommend the top three job posting templates that match best. Figure 7 shows an example where the user has asked for a “Project Manager with 3+ years experience”. In this example, Joule is in full-screen mode, giving space for details of a selected template to be shown on the right. The example shows that the user has clicked on the first suggestion in the list on the left.

Figure 7: Using Joule for SAP Fielglass to create a job posting for a contingent worker, with Joule providing top 3 matching job posting templates. Alt Text: Screenshot of Joule in full-screen mode, with prompts and outcome as described in the text.

Joule: Legal Entity Recognition

If Legal Entity functionality has been enabled in your SAP Fieldglass system, so that you can manage the external workforce for more than one company, you can now specify the legal entity as part of your requirement to Joule when creating job postings or statements of work. Figure 8 shows an example for creating a job posting for the legal entity “UK”.

Figure 8: Legal Entity Recognition by Joule for SAP Fieldglass when creating a job posting. Alt Text: Four Joule images, from left to right, with prompt “Create Job Posting”, Joule asks for details, then “programmer, legal entity is UK”, Joule shows 3 templates, user chose one, Joule asks to confirm, user says “yes”, Joule shows details for reviewing before creating.

Read more:

  • Documentation: Legal Entity Recognition in Joule.

Joule: Decision Form Approvals

When asking Joule for pending work items, Joule will now include Decision Form approvals too, as shown in Figure 9.

Figure 9: Joule for approving Decision Forms. Alt Text: Five Joule screenshots, user chooses “View my pending items”, Joule shows one “Contractor Vetting” pending approval, user selects “Approve Decision Form” from the context menu, Joule asks for a comment, user says “no comment”, decision form is approved.

More Information on Joule

Read this blog post and watch the video to learn more:

  • What’s New for Joule in SAP Fieldglass – November 2025.
  • Video: Introducing Joule in SAP Fieldglass (3:30 minutes).
  • Video: SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule (5:17 minutes).

 

Embedded AI for improved UX

AI-Assisted Skills-Based Hiring

When looking to hire consultants or contingent workers, you want to ensure that you get people with the right skills. Now, SAP Fieldglass makes this easy, with AI assistance.

AI helps you to specify which qualifications to ask for when creating a new job posting: AI-powered qualification recommendations uses AI to analyze your job title and description and then suggests relevant skills. The screen on the left in Figure 9 shows what this looks like.

AI helps you to consolidate qualifications that have different names for the same qualification. Suppliers describe the skills of their employees themselves, leading to a many different names for essentially the same skill. For example, “Azure Cloud,” “Azure”, and “MS Azure” could be used for someone with skills with Microsoft Azure Cloud. With the help of AI, these qualifications can be consolidated into one unique qualification. The screen top right of Figure 10 shows an example of how this is done.

Once skills have unique names, the other aspect that needs to be harmonized is the rating scale: some suppliers use 1 to 5, others perhaps 1 to 6 or 1 to 10. SAP Fieldglass normalizes these.

Having created a posting with appropriate skills, SAP Fieldglass helps you select candidates by making proposals based on the skills (and their ratings) that match, in addition to proposing candidates that have the appropriate job code. The image in Figure 10, bottom right, shows an example.

Figure 10: AI-powered qualification recommendations, qualification consolidation and skill-based candidate proposals in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Three screen snippets. On the left a list of 6 recommended qualifications for skills such as Excel, Powerpoint, Word, with a relevancy shown as a percentage (55%, 50%, …). Top right a list showing Qualification “Microsoft Azure” as heading, with two entries below “Azure Cloud” and “MS Azure” and buttons “Consolidate Qualifications” and “Cancel”. Bottom right a list of 4 potential candidates, their names greyed out, with the qualification matching percentage shown in the far right column.

Read more in this blog post:

  • Smarter Skills-Based Hiring with SAP Fieldglass

Automated Approvals for Resource Time Sheets

Another example where AI can make users much happier is approving resource time sheets. In industries that have high volumes of worker time submissions, manually and dealing with discrepencies in the submitted time sheets can be tedious and time consuming. Now, you can define tolerance limits so that hours that fall within the defined tolerances are automatically approved. This reduces manual effort significantly, freeing up users to do more value-adding activities.

Read more about it:

  • Documentation: Automated Approvals for Resource Time sheets Leveraging Tolerance Limits.

 

WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding

First-time users can often feel a bit lost when using new business software, no matter how easy it is to use – simply the fact that it is unfamiliar can make people feel uncomfortable.

This is where WalkMe comes in, helping users discover how to use the product. SAP Fieldglass now provides WalkMe content out of the box. Figure 11 shows how users can choose from a list of topics. Figure 12 shows how it looks like if a user starts a topic (the welcome pop-over shown on the left), and how WalkMe gives users guidance for each step in the process (the image on the right).

Figure 11: Starting WalkMe in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screenshot of the home page with a full-height pop-over on the right with heading “Type in your question” above a text entry field, below that sections “General”, “Contingent Workforce”, “Services” with a list of entries such as “Review work items” or “Create a job posting”.

Figure 12: WalkMe examples in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Two screenshots with pop-overs. On the left: “Review work items” with a description and a “Guide me” button; on the right “Approve Time Sheet” pop-over with a small popover connected to the “Approve” button, explaining what to do.

Find out more:

  • Documentation: WalkMe Walkthroughs Available in SAP Fieldglass.

 

Selected Usability Improvements in Applications

Applications in SAP Fieldglass are constantly improving, in many cases not just with new features and functions but also with aspects that improve the overall user experience. Even if they are only improvements in details: the details matter if you want to provide a good experience, since if you get them wrong, they can be very annoying.

Below are some highlights for these improvements in details.

Reusable Resource Types for faster resource creation
Rather than having to always manually enter all the data for a new contractor or piece of equipment, you can define those entries that you want to have pre-filled, by defining reusable resource types. For example you can define address, job code, time tracking, work schedule, activity checklist and more just once, and then use this to pre-fill your fields when creating a new resource. Find out more here:

  • Standardize Resource Creation with Reusable Resource Types in SAP Fieldglass

Tailored list views based on user roles
List views are core to daily operations for teams managing job postings, work orders, and statements of work. However, only some of the many columns shown are relevant to a user in specific role. Although users could customize their views, it turns out that this is rarely done. Now, customers can define views per role, specifying which columns to show and which to hide. This supports faster, more focused decisions and delivers a cleaner, more intuitive experience. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Customized List Views.

One-click line item copy for purchase orders
Here is a nice little detail that will make users smile: with one click, you can now copy the previous line item when creating a purchase order. In many procurement scenarios the same services are purchased regularly, so it is very helpful for users to copy an item and then make only the few necessary changes before continuing with the next item. As you can see in Figure 13, we have introduced a “copy” icon to duplicate the existing PO line.

Figure 13: One-click duplication of purchase order line. Alt Text: a screen snippet showing a PO line with the icon for copying the line on the left of the line.

Making it easier for suppliers to manage line items
We have introduced a number of improvements for suppliers working on SoWs (Statements of Work). For example, when purchasing contracts are in place, it makes sense to include information from the contract in a purchase order to ensure compliance, accurate pricing and so on. Navigating to the right information in the contract when creating a purchase order has been a challenge – but no longer! Suppliers can search and insert contract segments in any order now. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Supplier Workflow Enhancements on SOW Characteristics Tab.

Making it easier for suppliers to maintain charges against PO items
In asset-intensive and plant maintenance industries, suppliers frequently need to submit or resubmit charges against PO lines. This is especially common when working with complex SOWs that involve multiple tasks and services. Up until now, suppliers encountered multiple navigation paths when adding or correcting line items, particularly after a charge is rejected due to issues like incorrect pricing or missing details. Now, this has been made significantly easier: all relevant actions are accessible no matter where they begin the process. Figure 14 shows an example.

Figure 14: Supplier access to all actions when managing PO line charges. Alt Text: two screenshots, showing the card “Corrective maintenance repairs – Engineering Repairs Services and Parts” in different contexts, on the left with an “Edit” button, on the right with an “Itemize” button.

 

Further Information

For an overview of the major new features with the 2511 release, have a look at:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Spotlight Features Guide.

These videos are available too:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Configuration Manager
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - General Updates
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Services Procurement

Have a look at all the latest updates, month by month, in the

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.
  • This blog post explains how to use the What’s New Viewer:
    SAP Fieldglass November 2025 Release Communication Now Available!

Another good place to stay up to date is the SAP Fieldglass page on SAP Community.

 

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this sixth part, covering SAP Fieldglass.

I will post part seven next, covering latest UX innovations for spend management with SAP Ariba.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Warranty claim item rejection
entry.content: We have HANA 2000 for automobiles client, not having Fiori. We want to know after OC version creation, how can we reject a particular line item which is received as wrongly created line from our front-end system. Before OC version, we can validate this in SBOM check. Once a claim reached to ZB05, how to reject a particular line item and make sure no further processing will happen through program. If we apply rejection decision code, the line gets process in further t-codes.

entry.title: Re: Asset
entry.content:
HI ,

You have a NEW BAdI Implementation (created recently)

If you created a new implementation of one of the BAdIs:

  • No action is required.
  • SAP confirms that in such cases the message appears but:
    • It is not caused by an upgrade
    • You can hide / suppress the message

➟ Fix

Go to:
SPRO → Asset Accounting → Transactions → Maintain Message Types
and suppress Message AU176 at the required message level (warning/info).

 

see sap note 3297821 .

entry.title: CDS view for BP Tax number
entry.content:
How to access Business Partner Tax Number via CDS views


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS view for BP Tax number
entry.content:
The BP Tax Number field stores the value in table DFKKBPTAXNUM. CDS View I_BUSINESSPARTNERTAXNUMBER fetches data from table DFKKBPTAXNUM.

CDS Views I_CUSTOMER and I_SUPPLIER can also be used to get the tax numbers. These CDS views provide fields TaxNumber1, 2, ..., 5 which are filled with the values inserted in tax categories. There is also a field in these CDS Views for VATRegistration.

The steps to maintain tax numbers with Tax Categories in App Maintain Business Partner are as follows:

  1. Go to App Maintain Business Partner.
  2. Create or Open an existing BP in Business Partner (Gen.) role.
  3. Go to Identification Tab.
  4. Go to section Tax Numbers to maintain tax categories and its correspondng tax number.

Note: If the Tax Number maintained is for a country which is different than the BP country maintained, then KNA1 and LFA1 tables would not store tax numbers. Hence, in such scenarios, CDS Views I_CUSTOMER and I_SUPPLIER would return blank values

entry.title: Re: Inconsistency between ACDOCA and ANLC (AR01) after Ma...
entry.content: Thank you for the explanation. It is clear now that the Asset Engine performs its own calculation and overrides the XMANPROPVAL_PN field in the subledger views. However, I still need to resolve the discrepancy. Since the revaluation was posted in 2024 (which is already a closed fiscal year), and the difference has carried forward to 2025, I have two questions: Correction Method: Since 2024 is closed, is there any supported way (or a specific transaction/program) to align the ANLC totals with the ACDOCA balances without reopening the year? Or must the correction be done in the current open period using a specific Transaction Type? Alternative Reports: Does S/4HANA offer any standard Asset Accounting report that reads directly from ACDOCA line items instead of the ANLC ? I am looking for a report where the manual proportional values from the Universal Journal are respected and displayed as the final balance. Any advice on how to handle this 'proportional shift' for multiple assets would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks – Best Practices SQL Query Performance Tuning Tips
entry.content:
@N1kh1l - In a Zero‑Copy integration model, SAP Datasphere federates or shares data directly from SAP or non‑SAP systems (including Databricks Delta Lake) without moving or duplicating it. Hence Datasphere(DSP) manages logical partitioning and data organization at the semantic layer.


SAP Databricks does support Liquid Clustering, because SAP Databricks is a fully embedded OEM version of the Databricks platform inside SAP Business Data Cloud, and it uses the same Delta Lake engine that Liquid Clustering is built on.

entry.title: Is SAP ByD supporting CXMAL scenarios
entry.content:
Yes — you can use SAP Business ByDesign (ByD) to send e‑invoices to GEP SMART, but only if you generate and send them in cXML, because GEP SMART’s preferred direct‑connect integration format for invoices is cXML.

Below is a clear breakdown using the evidence collected:

  1. Does GEP SMART accept cXML invoices?

Yes.
Multiple GEP documents explicitly state that GEP SMART supports cXML for B2B invoice integration:

  • ConocoPhillips' GEP SMART integration guidelines confirm that GEP integrates suppliers for PO & invoice handling through cXML/PIDX, and that the preferred method for invoicing is cXML.

  • GEP’s own SAP Adapter documentation says it can convert SAP ERP transactional data into cXML and upload it to GEP SMART.
    [gep.com]

So the receiving side (GEP SMART) absolutely supports cXML invoices.

  1. Can SAP Business ByDesign send cXML invoices?

SAP Business ByDesign does not natively output cXML for customer invoices, but you can send cXML in two supported ways:

Option A — Integrate via SAP Business Network (Ariba-style cXML routing)

SAP Business Network explicitly supports cXML as an outbound routing method for invoices:

  • You can set the routing method for invoices to cXML, which means SAP ByD → SAP Business Network → Buyer (GEP SMART)*.

This requires:
✔ A Business Network (Ariba) supplier account
✔ cXML InvoiceDetailRequest mapping
✔ HTTPS endpoint configuration

If the buyer lets you connect through SAP Business Network to GEP SMART, this can work smoothly.

Option B — Use a middleware that generates cXML (recommended)

SAP ByD can send invoice data via SOAP/XML outbound services. This data can then be transformed into cXML by:

  • SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration)
  • A partner solution (e.g., sinsou cXML integration)

  • GEP SMART SAP Adapter (but this is for SAP ERP, not SAP ByD)

SAP ByD supports external invoice integration via electronic data exchange channels, so the architecture looks like:

SAP ByD → outbound SOAP/XML → middleware → cXML → GEP SMART (Direct Connect)

This is the most common setup for ByD + GEP integrations.

  1. Important Clarification

There is no direct native export of cXML from SAP Business ByDesign (unlike SAP ERP + GEP Adapter).
Therefore:

➤ Direct ByD → GEP SMART in cXML is not possible without middleware.

But

➤ ByD → Middleware → cXML → GEP SMART works and is fully supported.

  1. Final Answer

Yes, you can send e‑invoices from SAP Business ByDesign to GEP SMART in cXML format, but you must use either SAP Business Network or an integration/middleware layer to convert and deliver the cXML file, because ByD does not generate cXML natively.

GEP SMART accepts cXML invoices, so as long as your integration produces valid cXML, the connection is supported.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Custom Analytical Queries And Services; export no longer possible?
entry.content:
In the lastest Public Cloud update (SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602) the new "Custom Analytical Queries And Services" app was introduced for the creation of analytical queries and services. The former "Custom Analytical Queries" app is still available, but one is no longer able to create new queries. 

The problem with new app I am currently facing is that there seems to be no export functionality anymore. Previously it was fairly easy to export the resultset of my query to Excel oder CSV but this functionality is no longer available.

Do you know any workarounds how I get my query exported?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP Joule + SAP Logistics Management: When Visibility Becomes Conversational through AI
entry.content:
For years, logistics technology has focused on improving execution and increasing visibility. Dashboards became richer. Events became faster. Data became more connected. Yet one friction point remains remarkably consistent in warehouses and transportation control rooms:

getting answers quickly still requires navigation, interpretation, and system knowledge.

A new interaction model is emerging

With the introduction of Joule, SAP is moving toward a world where users do not only read logistics information — they converse with it.

Instead of:

  • opening multiple applications
  • filtering datasets
  • running reports
  • validating cross-references

users can simply ask and that changes adoption dynamics dramatically.

Why this is particularly powerful in SAP Logistics Management

SAP Logistics Management acts as a unifying layer across transportation, warehousing, and network signals.

It is already the place where:

  • events converge
  • context is built
  • and operational relevance is created

Adding conversational interaction on top of that context is a natural evolution.

What kinds of questions become easier?

In practical enablement scenarios, we see operational teams asking things like:

  • How many bins are currently within scope for this warehouse?
  • What is the volume of handling units at a specific work center?
  • Which flows are trending toward delay?
  • Where do we see concentration of exceptions today?

Traditionally, answering these requires familiarity with structure and navigation.

With Joule, interaction can become significantly more intuitive.

Why operations teams care

Because logistics is time sensitive.

Reducing the effort required to obtain a reliable answer means:

  • quicker prioritization
  • faster escalation
  • better coordination between warehouse and transport
  • and less dependency on specialized system knowledge

Even small time savings per decision multiply quickly in busy operations.

The hidden benefit: lowering the adoption barrier

When organizations evaluate new logistics platforms, complexity is often the unspoken concern.

A conversational layer helps by:

  • making systems feel approachable
  • reducing training anxiety
  • enabling occasional users
  • supporting new team members

This is not just productivity improvement. It is adoption acceleration.

What we are learning while working with LGM scenarios

In our work around SAP Logistics Management, we are closely observing:

  • which questions operational users ask most frequently
  • where conversational responses reduce navigation effort
  • how structured event context improves answer quality
  • what makes information actionable versus merely visible

These observations are proving extremely valuable in early customer discussions and POCs.

Seeing it makes a difference

When users witness conversational retrieval of operational data within their logistics context, discussions change from:

“What does this system do?”

to

“How quickly can we start?”

Imagine a morning shift in the warehouse.

The supervisor has just arrived on the floor. Inbound trucks are queuing, outbound waves are forming, and a labor reallocation decision must be taken quickly.

Traditionally, to understand the situation, the supervisor would:

  • open multiple warehouse or monitoring apps
  • filter by activity area
  • review queues
  • maybe call someone for clarification

It takes time. And often the picture is already changing.

Now consider the same moment with Joule available inside SAP Logistics Management.

SAP LGM - Basic Joule Prompt

Instead of navigating, the supervisor simply asks:

“How many bins are currently active in this warehouse (LGM)?”

Joule returns the number immediately, based on the operational context already available in LGM.

A follow-up question comes naturally:

“How many Warehouse Tasks are waiting in Work Center GI-ZONE, list them?”

Again, an answer is surfaced without moving across transactions, reports, or menus.

Within seconds, the supervisor understands workload concentration and can rebalance labor or escalate where needed.

Where this could lead

We are only at the beginning.

As event intelligence, orchestration logic, and AI interaction mature together, we may see logistics teams shift from:

monitoring → to understanding → to predicting → to acting.

#SAP #SAPLogisticsManagement #Joule #SupplyChainTechnology #DigitalLogistics

entry.title: NEW RELEASE: Mapping the Journey to SAP S/4HANA - A practical guide for senior IT leadership
entry.content:
What is SAP’s Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA?

SAP’s Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic, experience-based guide designed to help senior leaders, IT decision-makers, and project managers plan to justify and execute their transition from legacy SAP ERP systems to the next-generation SAP Cloud ERP or Business Suite. Drawing on insights from thousands of real transformation projects, the guide blends high-level strategic choices with actionable implementation guidance and practical tool recommendations.

At its core, this edition shows how SAP Cloud ERP is not just a technical upgrade but a cornerstone for broader digital transformation, one that can unlock future-ready business models, intelligence-enabled processes, and improved operational agility.

 

More Than a Technical Upgrade

For many organizations, the move to SAP S/4HANA represents one of the most significant transformation initiatives of the decade. Yet the most successful customers do not approach it as a simple system upgrade. They see it as an opportunity to modernize their enterprise core, simplify operations, and position their business for continuous innovation.

SAP’s Mapping Your Journey to SAP S/4HANA provides practical, experience-based guidance to help organizations navigate this transformation with confidence. Built on insights from thousands of customer projects across industries, the framework helps business and IT leaders make informed decisions, align stakeholders, and turn complexity into clarity.

At its heart, the journey to SAP Cloud ERP is about creating a future-ready foundation to compete in a rapidly changing world.

Start with Strategy: Aligning Business Ambition and Technology

A successful SAP Cloud ERP journey begins with a clear understanding of why the transformation matters to the business.

Rather than focusing solely on technical drivers, leading organizations align their S/4HANA roadmap with strategic priorities such as:

  • Improving end-to-end process efficiency and transparency
  • Enabling faster, data-driven decision-making
  • Supporting new business models and digital services
  • Reducing technical debt and operational complexity

SAP Cloud ERP, powered by SAP HANA’s in-memory technology, simplifies data models and enables real-time analytics directly within business processes. Combined with a modern user experience and embedded intelligence, it creates a platform designed to evolve alongside the business, and not to constrain it.

Choosing the Right Path: One Size Does Not Fit All

Every organization’s starting point is different. SAP recognizes this and offers multiple transition paths to SAP Cloud ERP, allowing customers to choose the approach that best fits their goals, risk profile, and timeline.

System Conversion: Ideal for customers who want to retain existing processes and configurations while moving to SAP Cloud ERP. This approach minimizes disruption and accelerates time to value, making it attractive for organizations seeking continuity with modernization.

New Implementation: Designed for customers ready to reimagine their business processes. By adopting standard best practices and reducing historical complexity, this approach enables greater simplification and long-term agility.

Selective Data Transition: A hybrid approach that allows customers to selectively move data, processes, or organizational units. It offers flexibility for complex landscapes, phased transformations, or mergers and divestitures.

The right choice depends on business ambition, regulatory requirements, and readiness for change, not just technical considerations.

 

Cloud as a Strategic Enabler

For many customers, the journey to SAP Cloud ERP goes hand in hand with a broader cloud strategy. SAP S/4HANA Cloud options enable organizations to shift from infrastructure management to innovation, benefiting from:

  • Scalability and operational resilience
  • Faster change management to adopt innovations
  • Predictable lifecycle management

Whether deployed on-premise, in the cloud, or in a hybrid model, SAP Cloud ERP provides the flexibility to evolve at a pace that aligns with business needs.

 

Laying the Foundation for Success

Technology alone does not deliver transformation. The guide highlights several critical success factors that consistently differentiate successful projects.

People, Skills, and Governance: Transformation requires strong sponsorship, cross-functional collaboration, and early investment in skills. Clear governance and change management ensure that transformation delivers lasting business impact, far beyond a technical go live.

Process Simplification and a Clean Core: SAP encourages customers to embrace standardization where it adds value and to reduce unnecessary custom code. A clean core approach lowers total cost of ownership, accelerates upgrades, and keeps the system ready for innovation.

Data and Custom Code Readiness: High-quality data is essential for intelligent processes. Preparing master data, archiving obsolete information, and analyzing custom code early reduces risk and avoids delays during migration.

 

Turning Insight into Action with SAP Tools

SAP provides a comprehensive set of tools and accelerators that support customers throughout their transformation journey, from early discovery to execution.

  • SAP Signavio solutions help customers understand how their current processes perform and where SAP Cloud ERP innovations can drive measurable improvement.
  • SAP Readiness Check offers a fact-based view of technical readiness, highlighting simplification items, add-on compatibility, and required adaptations.
  • Migration and planning tools help structure the transformation roadmap, ensuring transparency and predictability.

Together, these tools enable customers to make decisions based on data.


Conclusion: Building the Intelligent, Sustainable Enterprise

The journey to SAP Cloud ERP is a pivotal moment that goes beyond modernizing ERP, but to rethink how the enterprise operates and creates value.

With a clear strategy, the right transition path, and a strong focus on people and processes, organizations can turn transformation into a competitive advantage. SAP’s proven methodologies, tools, and ecosystem help customers move forward with confidence accelerating innovation while reducing risks.

Ultimately, SAP Cloud ERP is not the destination. It is the foundation for becoming an intelligent, sustainable enterprise — ready to adapt, innovate, and grow in the years ahead.

The document can be accessed via sap.com(AEM) or as PDF in the Digital Library

entry.title: Action Required: Update your certificate trust stores for enhanced SAP HANA Cloud security
entry.content:
To enhance the security of our cloud services, SAP is updating the root certificate authority (CA) for various services, including the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, SAP BTP, ABAP environment, and SAP HANA Cloud. We are transitioning our server certificates signed by DigiCert Global Root G2 and G1/G5 cross-signed CAs to the newer and more secure "DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5" CA, as DigiCert will stop signing new server certificates with the old root certificate.

This change is necessary to align with the latest industry security standards and recommendations, which call for stronger cryptographic keys. The new G5 certificate chain uses RSA-4096, providing a higher level of security for your connections.

What Does This Mean for You?

If you have client applications or services that connect to SAP HANA Cloud, you may need to take action to ensure a seamless transition and avoid connection failures.

Call to Action: Review the official notes and update your Trust Stores as needed

To prevent any disruption to your services, it is crucial that you update the trust stores for your client applications and services, as needed. Please review the officially provided SAP notes, giving more guidance:

  • SAP Note 3397584: HANA Cloud Connections will switch from "DigiCert Global Root CA" to "DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5"
  • SAP Note 3399573: HANA Cloud Switch of DigiCert Root Certificate

Rollout Timeline

The switch to the G5 root CA for existing SAP HANA Cloud regions is planned for the second quarter of this year. The timeline for your landscape will be communicated via established communication channels begin of March. This blog post will also be updated with further information once available.  

Further Information and References

As the root certificate change is not only impacting SAP HANA Cloud usage, but also other SAP BTP services, we highly recommend reviewing the below mentioned references/notes, to ensure the highest degree of business continuity:

  • SAP Note 3566727: Root Certificate Replacement in the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Blog Post: SAP BTP Cloud Foundry: Switching to higher security level Root Certificate Authority
  • Blog Post: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – New Root Certificate Authority
  • GitHub: SAP BTP Trust Store

To ensure your applications continue to operate smoothly and securely, we suggest you take the necessary steps to update your trust stores as soon as possible.

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series: From Accident Victim to Ironman 70.3 Finisher
entry.content:
My name is Vinit Kumar. I work as Development Architect in SAP Cloud Lifecycle Management (CLM) and I would like to share a bit about my hobby as part of the SAP Community Hobbies series. I’m an endurance athlete who discovered the incredible world of triathlon – a multi-sport discipline combining swimming, cycling, and running. What started as rehabilitation from a serious accident has evolved into my greatest passion. My hobby involves training for and competing in endurance events, with my crown achievement being the completion of Ironman 70.3 Goa.

 

I would love to share my journey of resilience and multi-sport endurance, culminating in becoming an Ironman 70.3 finisher in November 2022, which is one of my proudest moment.

 

The Journey

My journey began in the most unexpected way. In June 2014, I suffered a serious accident that left me with a fractured leg, metal plate, and 8 screws. For over a year, I couldn’t stand or walk for more than 30 minutes. It was my darkest period, both physically and mentally.

But sometimes adversity introduces us to our true selves. I decided to fight back with the philosophy that "pain will kill the pain." I started with jogging and running as therapy. The confidence booster for was to be able to complete a half marathon with implants in 2017.

The real spark came in May 2019 when I first heard about Ironman 70.3 Goa – this ultimate test of multi-sport endurance. The idea of earning the title "Ironman finisher" was intoxicating, even though I had no road cycling experience.

 

Why Ironman?

What I love most is the holistic nature of triathlon. It’s not just about physical strength – it builds mental resilience, discipline, and teaches you that limits are often self-imposed.

 

The diversity of training keeps things exciting: early morning swims, cycling through varied terrains, and the meditative rhythm of long runs.  But beyond that, it’s the personal transformation – going from someone who couldn’t walk 30 minutes to crossing an Ironman finish line after 7.5 hours.

The challenges

But also have to train the body and mind for endurance.

  • Physical setbacks: Starting with implants, then requiring another surgery to remove them, forcing me to restart from zero. I overcame this by viewing each setback as a new beginning rather than an end.
  • Skill gaps: Being new to road cycling was intimidating. I addressed this through consistent practice and learning from experienced cyclists.
  • Pandemic disruption: COVID-19 cancelled the 2020 and 2021 ironman Goa race, also made it difficult to maintain fitness and motivation. I stayed focused on my long-term goal and used the time to diversify my training.
  • Race day conditions: Ironman 70.3 Goa presented hot, humid conditions for my longest swim (1.9km in the sea) and longest bike ride (90km with 1100+ feet elevation gain). I overcame this by remembering: "pain is temporary, finishing is permanent."

The Learnings

  • Start where you are: I couldn’t walk 30 minutes – you don’t need to be fit to start getting fit.
  • Diversify your training: I added yoga, boxing, and strength training to build comprehensive fitness.

 

  • Embrace the "never give up" attitude: Discipline and persistence matter more than natural talent.
  • Focus on finishing, not winning: "Pain is temporary, finishing is permanent".
  • Build gradually: Respect the process and allow your body to adapt.
  • Find your community: Connect with fellow athletes to get invaluable guidance, support, and motivation.

 

Connection to Life @ SAP

Interestingly, triathlon and the SAP world share many parallels:

  1. Process Integration: Just like SAP integrates different business processes, triathlon integrates three distinct sports into one seamless experience.
  2. Performance Analytics: Both require constant monitoring and optimization – tracking training hours, performance metrics, and continuous improvement, similar to SAP’s focus on data-driven decisions.
  3. Systems Thinking: Success in both requires understanding how different components work together – in triathlon, how swim technique affects bike performance, and how bike effort impacts running, much like how SAP modules interconnect.
  4. Change Management: My journey from injury to Ironman mirrors organizational transformation – it requires planning, phased implementation, stakeholder buy-in, and persistence through challenges.
  5. Resilience and Continuity: Both demand building systems that can withstand disruption – whether it’s race day challenges or business continuity planning.

Remember, whether you’re implementing SAP solutions or training for an Ironman, the principle remains the same: "All it takes is all you got" If I can go from not being able to walk for 30 minutes to finishing an Ironman, imagine what you can achieve with dedication and the right mindset.

 

Thank You!

entry.title: From Sales Order to Carrier Award: Seamless Integration Between SAP LGM and BN4L
entry.content:
In today’s supply chains, transportation is no longer just execution — it is orchestration.

Organizations struggle with:

  • Disconnected ERP and freight planning
  • Manual tender emails
  • Lack of carrier transparency
  • Slow quotation cycles
  • Limited network collaboration

This is exactly where SAP Logistics Management (LGM) integrated with SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L) changes the game.

Let’s walk through a real end-to-end scenario — from Sales Order creation in S/4HANA to Carrier Award via BN4L — powered entirely on cloud-native integration.

1️⃣ Sales Order Creation in SAP S/4HANA

The journey begins with a Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA.

In this example:

  • Customer: LGM-CDL-01
  • Shipping Type: Direct LTL
  • Requested Delivery Date: 27.02.2026
  • Incoterms: DDP

This is pure ERP execution — but the key question is:

👉 How does this automatically trigger freight planning in the cloud?

2️⃣ Outbound Delivery Creation in S/4HANA

Once the outbound delivery is created:

  • Delivery 80000439
  • Total Weight: 500 KG
  • Goods Issue: Open

This delivery becomes the operational trigger for transportation planning.

3️⃣ Real-Time Integration to SAP Logistics Management (LGM)

You can see:

  • Integration task completed
  • Outbound Delivery 80000439 processed successfully
  • Business system mapped correctly

This confirms seamless S/4HANA → LGM integration.

No manual interface. No middleware customization. Cloud-native integration.

4️⃣ Logistical Demand Created in LGM

In LGM, the outbound delivery becomes a Logistical Demand.

Status:

  • Goods Issue: Open
  • Transportation Planning Status: Planned

This is the cloud abstraction layer: ERP document → Transportation demand object.

Now transportation planning begins.

5️⃣ Transportation Request in LGM

The system automatically creates a Transportation Request with:

 

  • Shipping Process: Direct LTL
  • Source: Palo Alto
  • Destination: Palo Alto
  • Weight & Volume details replicated

At this point: ✔ Consolidation is complete ✔ Carrier determination begins

6️⃣ Consignment Creation in LGM

The Transportation Request is converted into a Consignment.

Progress Overview:

  • Consolidation: Completed
  • Carrier Determination: In Tendering
  • Subcontracting: No Communication (yet)

Now we move into digital carrier collaboration.

7️⃣ Tender Creation in LGM

A Tender document (4200000024) is created in LGM.

Key Details:

  • Tendering Type: Peer-to-Peer
  • Currency: USD
  • Pickup & Delivery Dates defined
  • Response window: 5 hours

This is where LGM transitions from planning to network collaboration.

8️⃣ Publish Tender to SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L)

Fill the Request for Quotation Currency and Maximum Response time for carrier to respond to the shipper in SAP LGM.

 

With one click → Publish.

This pushes the RFQ to BN4L.

Behind the scenes:

  • Tender is converted to RFQ
  • Carrier is notified via SAP Business Network
  • Digital collaboration begins

9️⃣ RFQ Created in BN4L

 

In BN4L carrier tenant:

  • Freight RFQ is visible
  • Departure & arrival details shown
  • Stops clearly defined
  • SLA countdown running

Carrier now has structured visibility: ✔ No email attachments ✔ No PDF rate sheets ✔ Real-time collaboration

🔟 Carrier Fills and Submits Quotation in BN4L

In BN4L carrier tenant, carrier :

  • Reviews the RFQ document

Carrier proposes:

  • Total Amount: 989 USD
  • Pickup Date confirmed
  • Delivery Date confirmed

Carrier submits the quotation digitally.

1️⃣1️⃣ Tender Tile in LGM Shows “Quote Received”

 

Back in LGM: Lifecycle Status → Quote Received from Carrier

No manual updates. No phone calls. The network response is synchronized automatically.

1️⃣2️⃣ Awarding the Carrier

 Planner reviews:

  • Carrier price
  • Pickup confirmation
  • Delivery confirmation

 Click → Award

Digital award is sent back to BN4L.

1️⃣3️⃣ Carrier Assigned in Consignment – Process Complete

 

Consignment now shows:

✔ Consolidation: Completed ✔ Carrier Determination: Carrier Assigned

The freight planning cycle is complete.

From Sales Order to Carrier Award — fully digital.

🔎 What This Integration Truly Solves

Traditional ChallengeLGM + BN4L SolutionManual tender emailsStructured digital RFQLimited carrier visibilityReal-time collaborationNo SLA controlConfigurable response intervalsNo integration transparencyMonitor Business LogsDisconnected ERP & transportNative S/4 → LGM sync

🧠 The Bigger Picture

This is not just tendering.

This is:

  • ERP-driven freight planning
  • Cloud-native transportation orchestration
  • Network-powered carrier collaboration
  • Real-time lifecycle visibility
  • Scalable digital logistics architecture

LGM acts as the intelligent planning core. BN4L acts as the collaborative carrier network.

Together, they eliminate friction from transportation procurement.

🚀 Why This Matters for Mid-Sized & Enterprise Shippers

Whether you move:

  • LTL
  • FTL
  • Parcel
  • Multi-leg shipments

This integration ensures: ✔ Structured tendering ✔ Faster carrier responses ✔ Transparent award process ✔ Reduced freight lead time ✔ Improved procurement governance

#SAP #SAPLogisticsManagement #Joule #SupplyChainTechnology #DigitalLogistics

entry.title: Re: Using Adapter HTTP - Response 403 for OData API Post Method- How to solve the big challenge
entry.content:
Hi @Yogananda 

Very helpful blog !
What is worth to mention is that ODATA adapter handles X-CSRF token out of the box.


For HTTP POST method to on-premise ODATA API there is also nice SAP help page showing all steps required when using HTTP receiver adapter:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/sci/3361897820.html 

Two remarks:
- to get token and cookie, better is lightweight HEAD operation - avoids unnecessary data transfer
- reusing session between two HTTP calls (get token/cookie + modification operation) is a must, not an option (not only about performance)

br

Mateusz

entry.title: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error on OData $metadata in SAP Build Work Zone (BTP ABAP Trial)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am deploying a SAPUI5 application to SAP Build Work Zone using the Managed AppRouter, connected to the BTP ABAP Trial environment.

The app works perfectly locally in BAS, but in Work Zone, it fails to load data. The browser console shows an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when fetching the OData $metadata and Fiori annotations.

Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553100 [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed
Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553500 Error: HTTP request failed

My Configuration:

  • Destination has HTML5.DynamicDestination = true and WebIDEEnabled = true.

  • Destination has HTML5.ForwardAuthToken = true.

  • My xs-app.json route uses "authenticationType": "xsuaa".

  • I have already cleared the Work Zone site cache.

I have attached my manifest.json, routing file, and a screenshot of my Destination configuration for reference. Any help is greatly appreciated!

mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: zcpequipmentapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            - Name: zcpequipmentapp_html_repo_host
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: zcpequipmentapp_uaa
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
          existing_destinations_policy: update
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

  - name: zcpequipmentapp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - zcpequipmentapp.zip
          name: zcpequipmentapp
          target-path: resources/

  - name: zcpequipmentapp
    type: html5
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
      service-plan: application
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
      service-plan: app-host

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true


destination

entry.title: SLA Violation in embedded SAC (Sales & Service Cloud V2)
entry.content:
Hello Community, I want to report on Service Level Violation in the embedded SAC based on the data source “Case”.

The Scenario: If the date/time in Field “Initial Review Completed” is later than the SLA Milestone “Initial Review Due On”, this is a “SLA Violation”. This works fine with creating a new Dimension in the data source “Case” by comparing the two fields, however, if there is no date/time in “Initial Review Completed”, the value is “Null”, this is because the Case is not put into status ”In Process” yet, I found no way to mark this case as “SLA Violation” if the current time is later than the SLA Milestone “Initial Review Due On”. Is there somehow a solution to solve this on data source level or on story level in the embedded SAC?

kind regards,

Christian

entry.title: CDS-based Value Help: Company Code filter not prefilled from UI context (expected FR03)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
 
I created a custom field using the Custom Fields app (Key User Extensibility) in the business context Fixed Asset Master Data.
For value selection, I created a CDS-based value help using a consumption view entity with ObjectModel.dataCategory: VALUE_HELP.

Requirement
When the user opens the value help from the UI, the Company Code field in the value help dialog (restriction/filter area) should be automatically prefilled from the UI context.
Example: In the asset master screen, the Company Code is VR03, so the value help should open with Company Code = VR03 already set.

Actual behavior
The value help opens, but Company Code is empty in the restriction area, and the user has to manually enter VR03.
 
@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'GL Local Dimension Assignment'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP
@Search.searchable: true
@Consumption.ranked: true
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #VALUE_HELP_PROVIDER
define view entity YC_GLLOCALDIMENASSIGNVH
  as select from YI_GlLocalDimensionAssign
{
  @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CompanyCode'
  .defaultSearchElement: true
  key CompanyCode,

  @ObjectModel.text.association: '_Text'
  .defaultSearchElement: true
  key Assignment1,

  _Text,

  @Consumption.hidden: true
  _CompanyCode
}<br /><br />
Questions
How can I pass the UI context (Company Code) into a CDS-based value help created for a custom field?
Is there any annotation required (for example binding, filter mapping, or context inheritance) so the value help automatically uses the company code from the UI?
Is this context inheritance supported for Key User Extensibility custom fields, or do I need a different approach (for example a parameterized view, another value help type, or different extensibility mechanism)?
Any guidance or best practices would be highly appreciated.
Thank you

entry.title: Re: Asset
entry.content: Why the warning appears is because, SAP made technical changes in the depreciation calculation logic in S4HANA 2022. If you have implemented either FAA_DC_CUSTOMER or FAA_EE_CUSTOMER, the system issues a warning to ensure you verify that your custom logic is still valid. Even if you’ve implemented the BADIs correctly, the system continues to show the warning until you adjust or deactivate the implementation if it’s not needed. Check SAP note 3297821

entry.title: Re: Semi-finished materials in CK40N to pick up price fro...
entry.content:
In ECC, the standard costing programs CK11N/CK40N are designed to calculate costs bottom‑up (raw materials + activities + overhead), not simply pick up a price from the material master. That’s why your attempt with COPC0005 / EXIT_SAPLCK21_002 didn’t trigger — those exits are more about customizing cost component splits or valuation logic, not overriding the fundamental costing calculation.

Here’s the landscape of what’s possible:

1. Standard Behavior
Cost estimates for semi‑finished goods are always built from their BOM and routing, unless you explicitly mark them as non‑costed or use a special valuation strategy. The system does not natively “read” the planned or standard price from the material master for semifinished goods during costing runs.

2. User Exits / BADIs

  • COPC0005 (EXIT_SAPLCK21_002): Influences cost component assignment, not the valuation base price.
  • CK_F_CSTG / CK_F_COSTING BADIs: These are more relevant in S/4HANA, but in ECC they don’t exist in the same form.
  • In ECC, there is EXIT_SAPLCKDI_001 (component valuation) and EXIT_SAPLCKDI_002 (activity valuation). These can be used to override how materials/activities are valued, but they still expect you to provide a valuation logic, not just “read the price field.”

3. Workarounds

  • Valuation Variant Customizing: In transaction OKKN, you can define valuation strategies. For semifinished goods, you could set a strategy that picks up the planned price (field VPRS or ZPPR) or standard price instead of costing. This is the cleanest way if your business requirement is consistent.
  • Marking semifinished goods as purchased: If you treat them as externally procured (even if they’re not), the costing run can pick up the price from the material master instead of exploding the BOM.
  • Custom Enhancement: If you truly need to override costing logic, you’d have to enhance the valuation exits (like EXIT_SAPLCKDI_001) to fetch the material master price and return it as the valuation result.

4. Migration Consideration
Since you’re migrating one company code to another client with its own costing variant, this is the perfect time to adjust the valuation variant customizing. That way, you don’t rely on exits, and the costing run behaves consistently across environments.

entry.title: Re: New Certification Format for SAP SuccessFactors Time...
entry.content:
Hi @Arulgsenthil 

The new format of certification is part of the reimagined transformation in the era of AI, which moves away from the proctored exam with test-type questions and rather demonstrate your knowledge on more practical basis and overall understanding of the solution instead of memorizing questions. https://learning.sap.com/get-certified/reimagining-certification

For the formats, the certifications might be scenario-based or system-based, and there are significant differences between each. 

  • https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/scenario-based-assessment-faqs
  • https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/certification-support/system-based-assessments-faq

Now, for the SAP SuccessFactors Time Management certification (C_THR94), the new format is scenario-based, which means that you will be testing your knowledge with an AI roleplay, that will be asking questions about the different solution areas, such as Time Sheet, Time Management, business rules, etc.. and you will be evaluated based on your responses. 

There's another option if you do not wish to have an AI review. You can upload a recorded video presenting a use case and an SAP expert would review and evaluate your expertise.

Hope this helps to clarify. 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit WM Stock with Internal HU Number Range
entry.content:
Good day @melani_fatimah291 

Thank you for your question.

That is a limitation of the Warehouse Stock migration object in EXM migration, especially when trying to create the HU and load the stock already packed into that HU at once.

In practice, the migration object can create HU related data, but the stock may still be posted as bin stock and not end up assigned to the HU as you expect in one step. This is why you see the HU generate but stock not packed to it.

See the following references for more insights:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/b3fd91a776ea496fa79368613228fa8b.html

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2939112

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/e3674080435c4ab687a5dd82992260ec.html?locale=en-US

If your goal is stock in bin and packed in HU generated internally, you usually need to validate whether the migration objects support that exact combination in your release and template behaviour. If not, consider separating the steps for stock load and HU packing, rather than expecting one upload to fully pack stock into the newly generated HU.

Consider carrying out these checks:

  • In your migration template, verify how the Highest Level HU, Indicator: Stock Loaded to HU, stock item rows and HU reference consistency fields are filled.
  • Confirm whether your process expects existing HU number vs internally generated HU.
  • Test a small file with one bin, one material, one HU and explicit HU assignment fields populated.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - Certifictaion
entry.content:
Hi All,

I recently took this exam (C_BCBDC_2511), and the assessment is already available in new format. You have two options for choosing the type of evaluation method you chose:

option A: You can do AI-Role play with the AI the keeps on asking you questions and you can answer it as per the scenario AI Avatar gives you. This works as if you are being interviewed by someone.

Option B: You need to submit a whole presentation with explanation and once you submit, someone from SAP will evaluate your submitted documents and will certify you based on your submission.

Now remember this assessment is an open-book  assessment and during AI-Role play you are free to use any reference. I opted for option A(the AI-Role play) and it is very easy to crack the exam.

The following study material should be sufficient for this exam:
BDC100
BDCBW1
BDCBX

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2025, Fully-Activated Appliance , Canada...
entry.content:
Hello @viniri85 

one option would be to activate the Canada Best Practices (BPs) in client 100:
You will get errors in various scope items due to the "brownfield nature" of the system (and need to correct or ignore/de-scope them as needed for your business scenarios) but in general the activation can be done.

Another option would be to use client 400 (has US & CA activated already).
This is a "Best-Practice" client ie. it contains ONLY the  customizing that comes with the BPs. Configuration tables for areas that are not needed for BP activation and/or where no SAP BPs exist will be empty.

And a third option could be to create a new client (eg. 101, either merged client or Best Practice Client) and activate only US & CA there. Specifically for first-time activation in a merged client, this documentation might be helpful: https://help.sap.com/doc/d232618e2ddc4251b7d80ee539d60126/2025.000/en-US/BP_OP_ENTPR_S4HANA2025_12_Merged_Client_EN_US.pdf

And lastly, the BP activation guide contains lot of additional information: 
https://help.sap.com/doc/b4447b6727684f13917622cf63e1eb98/2025.000/en-US/AdminGuideS4HANAImpl_HELP_EN.pdf 

Hope this helps. 
Best, Joerg

What I’m trying to achieve:

I would appreciate guidance on:

entry.title: SAC Excel Add-in – Error 2120042 when inserting calculated measures after latest update
entry.content:
In the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Excel Add-in, an error occurs when inserting a calculated measure from an SAC model into Excel.

As soon as a calculated measure is added to the worksheet, the following error message appears:

Caught exception: exception 2120042: An unexpected error occurred.
For further information on this error search SAP notes for one of the following tags:
loc=0xe1380be9e058eda0,
ctx=0x25fc6d6118426d2b.
If you don't find an according note, open a support ticket and include the trace files containing correlation id 0xdc63fface67a4b4c.

Key Observations:

  • Standard (non-calculated) measures can be inserted and used without any issues.

  • Calculated measures previously worked correctly in the Excel Add-in.

  • The issue started occurring after the latest SAC update.

Correlation ID:
0xdc63fface67a4b4c

Expected Behavior:
Calculated measures should be inserted and function correctly in the Excel Add-in, as they did before the latest SAC update.

entry.title: Re: CBC : Set Up Integr Financial Planning * with "you ne...
entry.content:
Good day @Kraikanchit 

Thank you for your question.

That is an identity issue not a CBC configuration issue. The error means your email is already registered, so the system will not let you create a new account with the same email.

  • Consider resetting your password on the existing account.
  • Make sure your account is linked to the correct S-user/P-user.
  • Verify with your administration that your S-user is active, assigned to the correct company and required support content are assigned.
  • You may also try private window, clear your cookies and try login in again with your same account.
  • If it still does not work, then raise an access issue and describe your issue.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: See a complete end to end process diagram in signavio
entry.content:
Hi @jigar_patel42 

In SAP Signavio Process Manager, there is no automatic “full end‑to‑end” flattened view that expands all linked subprocess diagrams into one combined diagram. Linked subprocesses can only be opened individually via the “+” icon in the model or Collaboration Hub, rather than displayed as a single integrated diagram. 

If you want everything in one visual, the only option is to model a high‑level diagram using expanded subprocesses, but this must be built manually. Here's the documentation that can help you with creating sub-processes - https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/user-guide/subprocesses 

Hope this helps. 

Best,
Shreenith

entry.title: Re: No transfer of interval 4000000000 4000000020 for obj...
entry.content:
That error message means the number range interval for object BU_PARTNER is not correctly transferred or available in your client. In other words, when you try to create a Business Partner, the system is looking for the interval 4000000000 – 4000000020, but it hasn’t been set up or transported properly.

Why it happens
Business Partner number ranges are client-dependent. They don’t get transported automatically with customizing requests. If you migrated or copied settings between clients, the number range interval may not exist in the target client. The grouping you chose (0001 Int Number Assignment) is tied to a specific number range interval. If that interval isn’t defined, you’ll see this error.

How to Fix
Check Number Range Customizing via transaction BUCF, verify that interval 4000000000 – 4000000020 exists for object BU_PARTNER, if not, create it manually.

Check Grouping Assignment
In IMG > SAP Business Partner > Basic Settings > Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges. Here, ensure, you have maintained the correct range to the required Grouping

Transport Consideration
Remember: number ranges are not transported. You must manually maintain them in each client.

Consistency Check
After maintaining, run BP creation again. The error should disappear once the interval exists and is linked to your grouping.

entry.title: Re: Rework Production order, its component level price di...
entry.content:
This is a classic dismantling / rework costing scenario, and SAP does not provide a direct “split moving average price across BOM components” function out of the box. But you can model it with a combination of production order logic, settlement rules, and valuation strategies.

How it works in real time

Rework / Dismantling Order (CO07)
You create a rework production order for the hypothetical dismantling material. The purchased product (valued at moving average price) is issued to the order. The BOM of the dismantling material contains the components you want to receive back.

Goods Movements
Issue the purchased product to the order (movement 261). Receive the dismantled components back (movement 531). At this point, the order collects the cost of the issued product.

Cost Distribution
The cost of the dismantled product is held in the order. Settlement rules determine how that cost is distributed to the received components. You can configure settlement to distribute costs percentage-wise to each component (based on BOM percentages or manually defined settlement shares).

Settlement (KO88)
When you settle the order, the moving average price of the dismantled product is split according to the percentages and posted to the components. This way, each component gets a share of the dismantled product’s cost, and they can then be sold at that valuation.

entry.title: Re: PM-Revisions not seen in OIOB
entry.content:
Key checks to resolve

Plant Assignment
Revisions are plant‑specific. Make sure the revision you created is tied to the correct plant. If you created it without a plant or with a different plant, OIOB won’t display it when you log in under another plant context.

Validity Dates
Check the validity period of the revision in T352R. If the “valid from” date is in the future, OIOB won’t show it until that date is reached.

Authorization / View Settings
Sometimes, authorizations or user parameters filter what you see in OIOB. Verify that your user has access to the plant and revision group.

Consistency Check
Run transaction OIOB again and ensure you’re in the same client and plant where the entry was created. If still missing, run OIOB → Utilities → Display Table Entries to confirm the customizing view is consistent with T352R.

Transport vs. Local Entry
Revisions are customizing objects. If you created it in one client but are checking in another, it won’t appear unless transported. Double‑check whether you’re in the same client where you made the entry.

entry.title: Re: JMS Mastery Series Part 2 - The JMS Revolution
entry.content:
Great content @MateuszPiotrowski .
One small point regarding Point 6 (“If retries exhausted: Message moves to DLQ”). In CPI, JMS does not have native retry‑exhaustion handling. Messages won’t move to DLQ unless there is a system‑level issue such as a tenant crash or broker recovery failure. For business‑level failures, retry limits must be implemented manually using the JMS retry header.

entry.title: Re: Creating Number Range Objects in SAP BTP Trial Account?
entry.content:
In SAP BTP Trial you cannot create standard Number Range Objects like in on-premise systems using SNRO. Trial accounts have limited admin access so full ABAP dictionary transactions are not available.

Workarounds include using a custom table with logic in your CAP or ABAP application to generate numbers or using database sequences. For production-ready number ranges a full BTP or on-premise system is required.

All numbering in the trial must be handled within your application.

entry.title: Re: Inconsistency between ACDOCA and ANLC (AR01) after Ma...
entry.content: Thank you for the explanation. It is clear that the Asset Engine performs its own calculation and overrides the XMANPROPVAL_PN field in the subledger views. However, I still need to resolve the discrepancy. Since the revaluation was posted in 2024 (which is already a closed fiscal year), and the difference has carried forward to 2025, I have two questions: Correction Method: Since 2024 is closed, is there any supported way (or a specific transaction/program) to align the ANLC totals with the ACDOCA balances without reopening the year? Or must the correction be done in the current open period using a specific Transaction Type? Alternative Reports: Does S/4HANA offer any standard Asset Accounting report that reads directly from ACDOCA line items instead of the ANLC (compatibility views)? I am looking for a report where the manual proportional values from the Universal Journal are respected and displayed as the final balance. Any advice on how to handle this 'proportional shift' for multiple assets would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Scope item's in Test System
entry.content:
Dear,

In a 3SL system it's not possible to create any workspace using test system.

All the workspace activities can be done only in Customizing and the content has to be moved from Customizing to Test system through Transports.

Once the "Deploy scope and Organizational Structure" is deployed the content from CBC system is deployed to respective S/4 system and automatically the TR will be generated.

The respective TR has to be released through "Export Customizing Transports" app and has to be imported into the Test system through "Import Collections" app.

Thanks,

SAP support

entry.title: Scheduling of Operations Across Multiple Material Codes
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team

We have three material codes for the same model with different operation sequences:

  • HALB0001 – 3 operations
  • HALB0002 – 4 operations
  • HALB0003 – 5 operations

We would like to schedule specific operations for the following work centers: WC0001, WC0002, and WC0004.

Our question is:
Is it possible to perform scheduling for these work centers without splitting the material codes? If yes, kindly guide us on how this can be configured in the system.

For your reference, please find the operation details below:

HALB0001
0010 – WC0001
0020 – WC0002
0030 – WC0005

HALB0002
0010 – WC0003
0020 – WC0004
0030 – WC0006
0040 – WC0007

HALB0003
0010 – WC0008
0020 – WC0009
0030 – WC0004
0040 – WC0002
0050 – WC0004

We appreciate your guidance on the feasibility and recommended approach.

entry.title: Re: Setup VAT deferred tax for Switzerland not available
entry.content: Yes it seems, there is no standard deferred tax solution for Switzerland in S/4HANA Public Cloud today. The practical workaround is to use custom tax codes with interim accounts and transfer VAT at payment, or build a side‑by‑side automation on BTP. For long‑term compliance, it’s best to raise this gap with SAP through the Customer Influence program so Switzerland gets added to the supported countries list.

entry.title: Re: Table T7BRT0 missing
entry.content: You need to maintain a valid entry in T7BRT0 that covers the payroll date (31.10.2018). Use SM30 → V_T7BRT0 to add the missing record with the correct validity period and tax parameters.

entry.title: Re: material group
entry.content:
Hi @adam_crane ,
My friend sap won’t automatically let one material “consume” another, but there are standard mechanisms that achieve the effect you want—depending on how strict your business rules are and how much you can adjust master data or BOMs.

My recommended approach (based on your description)
Since you said:
The material group “prevents procurement”
It’s a formality
Another company must fix it but is slow
You want all consumption to use a different material

The cleanest and fastest solution is:
Use Follow‑Up Material or BOM Replacement (CS20)
These require no cooperation from the other company and are fully standard.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One (SQL) – Unique Users with Assigned L...
entry.content:
Hi Krishna,

from my experience:

1) no
2) no
3) no. In FP 2602 there is a new global license view in ControlCenter but I don't think it is queryable
4) the user license assignment information is stored in file b1upf.xml on license server. You can extract the information from there in structured way. however in recent ersions the usernames are encrypted and you need to disable the encryption to be able to read in clear text.
